findings.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy
findings.description: The Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header is missing, exposing the application to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and clickjacking attacks.

findings.title: Missing X-Frame-Options Header
findings.description: The X-Frame-Options header is missing, rendering the application vulnerable to Clickjacking attacks.
findings.recommendation: Configure X-Frame-Options to DENY or SAMEORIGIN.

findings.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
findings.description: The X-Content-Type-Options header is missing, allowing browsers to MIME-sniff response content types.
findings.recommendation: Set the X-Content-Type-Options header to nosniff.

findings.title: Missing Referrer-Policy Header
findings.description: The Referrer-Policy header is missing, which could leak sensitive information in referrer URLs to third parties.

findings.title: Missing Permissions-Policy Header
findings.description: The Permissions-Policy header is missing, which controls browser features and APIs available to the page.

findings.title: Insecure HTTP Method Enabled: PUT
findings.description: The web server accepts the PUT method on the root URL, which can be misused by attackers or indicates improper method access controls.
findings.recommendation: Disable the PUT HTTP method on the web server if not strictly required.

findings.title: Insecure HTTP Method Enabled: DELETE
findings.description: The web server accepts the DELETE method on the root URL, which can be misused by attackers or indicates improper method access controls.
findings.recommendation: Disable the DELETE HTTP method on the web server if not strictly required.

findings.title: Insecure HTTP Method Enabled: PATCH
findings.description: The web server accepts the PATCH method on the root URL, which can be misused by attackers or indicates improper method access controls.
findings.recommendation: Disable the PATCH HTTP method on the web server if not strictly required.

findings.title: HSTS Max-Age Too Low
findings.description: The Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) max-age is set to less than 1 year (31,536,000 seconds), which does not satisfy industry standards.
findings.recommendation: Increase Strict-Transport-Security max-age directive to at least 31536000.

allFindings.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy
allFindings.description: The Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header is missing, exposing the application to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and clickjacking attacks.

allFindings.recommendation: Disable the DELETE HTTP method on the web server if not strictly required.

allFindings.description: The web server accepts the PATCH method on the root URL, which can be misused by attackers or indicates improper method access controls.
allFindings.recommendation: Disable the PATCH HTTP method on the web server if not strictly required.

allFindings.title: HSTS Max-Age Too Low
allFindings.description: The Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) max-age is set to less than 1 year (31,536,000 seconds), which does not satisfy industry standards.
allFindings.recommendation: Increase Strict-Transport-Security max-age directive to at least 31536000.

allFindings.title: Sensitive Directories Protected
allFindings.description: None of the tested paths returned 200 or 403 status codes.

allFindings.title: Reflected XSS Checks Passed
allFindings.description: No unescaped reflection of scripting payloads was detected in the tested input fields.
allFindings.evidence: Probed parameters q, search, name, input with standard XSS script alert.
allFindings.recommendation: Ensure strict contextual output escaping is maintained.

allFindings.title: SQL Injection Checks Passed
allFindings.description: No database query errors or SQL syntax leaks were detected in response to dynamic probes.
allFindings.evidence: Probed parameters id, user, username, query, q, search, product with 4 SQL injection sequences.
allFindings.recommendation: Continue enforcing SQL parameterization across all query operations.

allFindings.title: Open Redirect Checks Passed
allFindings.description: No arbitrary redirection to external evil domains was detected during parameters probing.
allFindings.evidence: Tested 8 typical redirect parameters with external domain payload.

allFindings.title: CORS Security Check Passed
allFindings.description: No wildcard ACAO, origin echoing, or credentials leakage was detected.
allFindings.evidence: Tested normal request and requests with Origin header overrides.

allFindings.title: No Session Cookies Set
allFindings.description: The target endpoint did not issue any Set-Cookie headers in the audited response.
allFindings.recommendation: Ensure cookies continue to be avoided if session tokens are handled via custom headers.

allFindings.title: Server Banner Leaks Checked
allFindings.description: No Server or X-Powered-By information banners were detected in response headers.
allFindings.evidence: Headers Server and X-Powered-By are absent.

log: [WAPT] Initializing Passive Security Scan for: https://facebook.com
